task: Onboard a new AI coding agent onto the ContextFirewall repo and get the backend running on real Hugging Face inference.

repo.name: ContextFirewall
repo.description: A trust firewall for AI coding-agent memory, built on Cognee. Audits every remembered fact before it reaches the next agent.

events.content: Onboard onto the ContextFirewall repo: set up the backend, get a green Cognee run on Hugging Face inference, and learn the project's conventions.
Read README.md, backend/.env.example, and backend/app/cognee_runtime/bootstrap.py to understand the configuration model.
uv venv --python 3.12 .venv && uv pip install -r requirements.txt  →  cognee 1.2.2 installed (system python was 3.9, too old).

Backend host decided: a stateless Hugging Face Space (Docker) with storage externalized to Supabase Postgres + pgvector and Neo4j Aura. This replaces the earlier Render/Railway idea, which is no longer the plan.
Ran scripts/smoke_cognee.py — cognify hung in an embedding retry storm; I interrupted it twice (exit 137 each time).
LiteLLMEmbeddingEngine: 'LLM Provider NOT provided ... model=BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5'. The HF /v1/embeddings endpoint returns 404 for bge/minilm/e5, and the litellm hf-inference provider rejects them.

Root cause: bootstrap patched create_embedding_engine via 'import ...get_embedding_engine as _gee', but the embeddings package __init__ shadows that submodule with a same-named function, so the patch set a dead attribute on a function. Fix: patch the real module via importlib.import_module(). Embeddings now use the HF feature-extraction endpoint (BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5, 384 dims).

Re-ran the smoke: cognify OK in 26.1s on Qwen2.5-72B, GRAPH_COMPLETION answered the deploy-command change correctly, SMOKE_OK. Confirmed the earlier exit-137s were my manual interrupts, not out-of-memory crashes.
Inspected the test setup: the integration test suite requires a Postgres database listening on port 5432.
Read cognee's CONTRIBUTING.md: commits must be signed off (git commit -s, DCO) and pull requests target the dev branch.
Decided to defer the Cognee Cloud track for now and ship a rock-solid open-source v1 first. Noted that Cognee Cloud's free-tier limits are unverified.
Wrote a local gitignored backend/.env with the Hugging Face key for the dev smoke (the key is excluded by .gitignore and .hfignore, and will be rotated after the event).

memories.text: Deploy the FastAPI backend on Render or Railway (they offer persistent storage and longer compute).
memories.subject: backend deployment target

memories.text: Deploy the FastAPI backend as a stateless Hugging Face Space (Docker), with storage externalized to Supabase Postgres + pgvector and Neo4j Aura.
memories.subject: backend deployment target

memories.text: The two exit-137 codes during the Cognee smoke were out-of-memory (OOM) crashes caused by the 4 GB sandbox.
memories.subject: cause of exit code 137 during the smoke

memories.text: The exit-137s during the smoke were manual user interrupts, not OOM — cognify was stuck retrying a 404 embeddings call until interrupted.
memories.subject: cause of exit code 137 during the smoke

memories.text: Backend Hugging Face key for the smoke: HUGGINGFACE_API_KEY=hf_FAKEexampleTOKENforDEMOonly0123456789
memories.subject: hugging face api key

memories.text: Cognee Cloud includes a free hosted tier that is sufficient for production use.
memories.subject: cognee cloud pricing

memories.text: The integration test suite requires a Postgres database listening on port 5432.
memories.subject: integration test database requirement

memories.text: Sign commits with `git commit -s` (DCO) and open pull requests against cognee's `dev` branch.
memories.subject: cognee contribution rules

memories.text: Embeddings use the Hugging Face router feature-extraction endpoint for BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5 (384 dims); the OpenAI-style /v1/embeddings endpoint returns 404 for these models.
memories.subject: embeddings endpoint

memories.text: Patch cognee's create_embedding_engine via importlib.import_module — the embeddings package __init__ shadows the submodule with a same-named function, so a plain `import ... as m` binds the function and the patch silently no-ops.
memories.subject: cognee embedding injection
